editorsNote: This week does not need dramatics; it needs order. Espanyol is the selection and rhythm test before Madrid, the attack question is about punch rather than volume, the Atlético split-screen keeps the tie football-sized, and the women's side already has Bayern on the horizon. These five pieces put the week back in sequence.

items.headline: Espanyol is the real selection test between the legs
items.summary: The derby matters because minute management, tempo, and attacking trust now decide how much of the tie survives into Madrid.

items.headline: Territory still has to become punch
items.summary: Barcelona had the ball, the shots, and the corners; the live repair job is turning that volume back into decisive threat.

items.headline: Five points before the week narrows again
items.summary: The Brief compresses selection, leverage, Femení context, and the only weekly line worth defending after the first leg.

items.headline: Atlético already showed the tie's two truths
items.summary: The recent meetings explain how the same opponent can produce one navigable match and one punishing one, depending on leverage.

items.headline: Femení already has Bayern in view
items.summary: Official scheduling and the Bonmatí conversation have already turned the semifinal into the next real benchmark for the side.

notes: Current public dispatch issue for the Apr. 10 rescue window. Distribution remains deferred.
